abstract: Internalizing religious values through a structured "Faithful Friday" (Jum’at Beriman) culture serves as a strategic intervention for enhancing student character. This study investigates the specific religious values embedded in this weekly tradition and their impact on character strengthening at Jalaluddin Vocational School (SMK), Wonosobo. Utilizing a qualitative field research design with a case study approach, data were gathered through observations, interviews, and documentation involving school leadership, teachers, and students. The findings reveal that the Faithful Friday program incorporates activities such as Quranic recitation, congregational Dhuha prayers, Tahlil, and Mujahadah. These practices successfully internalize core religious values, specifically values of faith (aqidah), worship (ibadah), and wisdom (kearifan). The systematic integration of these values fosters essential character traits in students, including religious devotion, social awareness, environmental care, peacefulness, responsibility, and leadership skills. This school culture effectively bridges the gap between theoretical religious education and practical daily behavior, offering a model for holistic character development in vocational education settings.
